description.p: XDrawChem is a full-featured two-dimensional molecule drawing program for Linux and other Unix-like systems. It is similar in functionality to commercial tools such as ChemDraw and provides file compatibility with many chemical formats through OpenBabel.

ul.li: Fixed-length, fixed-angle bond drawing with automatic figure alignment
Ring library including amino acids, nucleic acids, aromatics, and sugars
Stereo bonds (wedge-up, wedge-down, wavy) and dative (coordinate covalent) bonds
Curved arrows and Bézier arrows for reaction mechanism drawing
MDL Molfile, CML, and ChemDraw XML format support
OpenBabel integration for 20+ additional chemical file formats
SMILES, canonical SMILES, and InChI string generation
IUPAC name lookup via PubChem REST API
PubChem structure browser integration
Valence checking and molecular formula calculation
13C NMR and IR spectrum prediction
ACS publication style preset for journal-ready figures
Export to PNG, PDF, and SVG; copy as SVG or high-DPI PNG to clipboard

screenshot.caption: Drawing organic molecules with ring templates and stereo bonds

ul.li: Qt6 + CMake build system (requires Qt 6.2+)
New: dative (coordinate covalent) bond type
New: ACS publication style preset
New: improved SVG export via Qt6 QSvgGenerator
New: copy as SVG or 300 dpi PNG to clipboard
New: canonical SMILES output via OpenBabel
New: IUPAC name lookup via PubChem REST API
New: PubChem structure browser integration
New: valence checking
New: PDF export
New: Georgian translation
Fixed: double bond geometry, molecule orientation on clean-up, curved arrow tip alignment, Bézier arrow drawing, SMILES output

description.p: First release candidate for XDrawChem 2.0 with Qt6 and CMake support.
